Title: Modular assembly of an artificially concise biocatalytic cascade for the manufacture of phenethylisoquinoline alkaloids

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates TpCAR to holo-TpCAR, enabling aldehyde formation and downstream PEIA production
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BsSfp was coexpressed with TpCAR to activate CAR in vivo. 2. Activated holo-TpCAR enabled conversion of phenylpropionic acids to aldehydes required for PEIA scaffold formation. 3. BsSfp-supported CAR function was part of the productive PEIA moiety module in E. coli.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: coexpression in pathway module; in vitro/in vivo biocatalysis; product detection by HPLC
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as an accessory activation enzyme rather than the primary metabolic determinant.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves methylation capacity by supplying SAM for OMT/NMT modules
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EcMAT was introduced into the MT/OMT module as part of a SAM supply system. 2. It synthesizes SAM from ATP and L-methionine to support methyltransferase reactions. 3. Its use supported production of methylated PEIA intermediates and products in vivo.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: module construction and whole-cell biotransformation; HPLC product analysis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Support gene for cofactor regeneration, not the main subject of engineering.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Relieves SAH inhibition in methyltransferase modules, supporting PEIA methylation and product formation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MmSAHH was coexpressed with EcMAT in the SAM supply system. 2. It hydrolyzes SAH, an inhibitor of O-methyltransferases. 3. This support function aided methylation-dependent synthesis of PEIAs in engineered E. coli.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: module construction and whole-cell biotransformation; HPLC product analysis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces phenylpropionic acids to the corresponding aldehydes in situ
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies immediate aldehyde precursor pool for PEIA scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmaceutical alkaloid scaffold and precursor to colchicine-related compounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables in situ aldehyde generation needed for efficient synthesis of PEIA intermediates and final products
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in E. coli whole-cell catalyst
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: purified enzyme assay; module reconstitution in vitro; whole-cell biocatalysis in vivo; HPLC
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TpCAR was selected because of its broad substrate scope. 2. Together with NCS, it efficiently converted substrate 1 and dopamine to (S)-1b without notable accumulation of aldehyde intermediate 1a. 3. TpCAR formed the upstream entry point of the concise PEIA cascade in vitro and in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7 promoter, constitutive induction system after IPTG induction in E. coli expression plasmids
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream biocatalyst to supply reactive aldehydes from accessible acids for modular alkaloid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: whole-cell biocatalysis; one-pot multi-step cascade; Mg2+, ATP, NADPH-regeneration conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Aldehyde intermediate can be reduced to alcohol by endogenous host enzymes unless host is engineered
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Purified holo-TpCAR was obtained after activation by BsSfp.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes Pictet-Spengler condensation of phenylpropionic aldehydes with dopamine to form the chiral PEIA backbone
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms core scaffold precursor directly leading to final PEIA products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmaceutical alkaloid scaffold and precursor to colchicine-related compounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Forms the PEIA core skeleton with high efficiency and supports broad substrate scope
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in E. coli; better expression than CjNCS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: purified enzyme assay; comparison with CjNCS; whole-cell biocatalysis; chiral/HPLC product analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TfNCS and CjNCS both enabled PEIA backbone formation from substrate 1 and dopamine with TpCAR. 2. TfNCS was chosen for further work because it showed better expression in E. coli. 3. TfNCS supported efficient and stereoselective formation of PEIA intermediates across a broad aldehyde substrate range.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core scaffold-forming enzyme for modular synthesis of natural and unnatural PEIAs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Depends on upstream aldehyde availability and downstream methyltransferase compatibility
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study notes NCSs strictly recognize the amine moiety but accept diverse aldehydes.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group to hydroxyl groups on PEIA intermediates using SAM
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for N-methylation to final PEIAs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: rate-limiting for (S)-autumnaline production with substrate 12; limited by poor activity/regioselectivity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmaceutical alkaloid scaffold and precursor to colchicine-related compounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Efficiently O-methylates many PEIA intermediates and is required before CNMT*-mediated N-methylation, but performs poorly on the autumnaline precursor substrate
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in E. coli whole-cell catalysts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: enzyme screening; one-pot cascade; strain optimization; time-course whole-cell biotransformation; HPLC; NMR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RnCOMT showed much higher activity than GsOMT1 toward (S)-1b and was selected for the general cascade. 2. O-methylation by RnCOMT had to occur before CNMT*-catalyzed N-methylation to yield (S)-1d. 3. RnCOMT supported efficient synthesis of many unnatural PEIAs but showed poor activity/regioselectivity on (S)-12b, making O-methylation the bottleneck for autumnaline production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful plug-in OMT for broad PEIA derivative synthesis, but should be replaced for substrates such as the autumnaline precursor
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: one-pot two-step and three-step whole-cell biocatalysis; dependence on SAM supply
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuity causes side effects on dopamine and poor regioselectivity, including formation of a 7-methoxyl positional isomer of (S)-1c
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Explicitly identified as the rate-limiting o-methylation step later replaced by plug-and-play strategy for autumnaline synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group to the amine of O-methylated PEIA intermediates using SAM
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms final PEIA product from immediate precursor
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not the main bottleneck initially, but wild-type activity was weak and required engineering
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmaceutical alkaloid scaffold and precursor to colchicine-related compounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Wild-type CNMT showed weak N-methylation activity toward (S)-1c; engineered CNMT* greatly enhanced final product formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in E. coli whole-cell catalysts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: enzyme screening; crystal-structure-guided mutagenesis based on PDB 6GKY; combinatorial engineering; docking; whole-cell product assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among screened NMTs, only CNMT displayed detectable activity toward (S)-1c. 2. Structure-guided engineering produced CNMT* (N92A/F332A), increasing activity up to 17.26-fold over wild type. 3. CNMT* enabled efficient final N-methylation in the concise PEIA cascade and improved (S)-1d production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering target for increasing N-methylation efficiency in PEIA and related alkaloid biocatalysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: depends on prior O-methylation and SAM supply
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Still insufficient for highest autumnaline titer; further engineering may be needed
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only substrates with correct O-methylation pattern were accepted; CNMT* only catalyzes the substrate with 6-OMe and 7-OH.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: (S)-1c and related correctly O-methylated PEIA intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Engineered N-methyltransferase catalyzing amine methylation of O-methylated PEIA intermediates
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms final PEIA product from immediate precursor
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key improved catalytic step for productive N-methylation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmaceutical alkaloid scaffold and precursor to colchicine-related compounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly improves N-methylation efficiency and enables efficient production of final PEIA products in vitro and in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in E. coli whole-cell catalysts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: alanine scanning; semi-rational design; combinatorial mutagenesis; docking; whole-cell biocatalysis; HPLC
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CNMT* (N92A/F332A) was identified as the best CNMT variant with 17.26-fold higher activity than wild type. 2. Docking suggested improved hydrogen bonding and enlarged substrate pocket as mechanistic basis. 3. CNMT* enabled efficient production of (S)-1d in the artificial four-enzyme cascade.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates value of structure-guided engineering to upgrade weak plant alkaloid NMTs for synthetic cascades
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: whole-cell one-pot three-step biocatalysis; SAM supply system required
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Still may require further engineering for efficient autumnaline production from substrate 12
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named CNMT* in the paper; accepts only the regioisomer with 6-OMe and 7-OH.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Artificial PEIA biosynthetic cascade / autumnaline branch
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: OMT module for autumnaline precursor conversion
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes O-methylation of PEIA intermediates using SAM
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor of (S)-autumnaline for downstream N-methylation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: o-methylation of the autumnaline pathway is a rate-limiting bottleneck; wild-type GsOMT1 better than RnCOMT on (S)-12b but still insufficient
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: colchicine precursor; pharmaceutical alkaloid pathway intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides a more suitable O-methylation activity than RnCOMT for the autumnaline precursor, but wild-type activity remains too low for high titer production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in E. coli; low expression without sumo tag
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: enzyme assays with purified protein; docking with AlphaFold2 model; mutant construction; whole-cell biotransformation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsOMT1 was revisited because RnCOMT poorly converted (S)-12b in autumnaline synthesis. 2. Wild-type GsOMT1 had better activity than RnCOMT toward (S)-12b but still produced low levels of (S)-12c. 3. This established GsOMT1 as the plug-and-play replacement target for overcoming the O-methylation bottleneck.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Native plant OMT useful starting point for targeted engineering of the autumnaline branch
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: one-pot three-step whole-cell biocatalysis; SAM supply system required
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low activity and low expression in E. coli; required further engineering and solubility tag
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Original OMT from the natural colchicine pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Artificial PEIA biosynthetic cascade / autumnaline branch
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: OMT module for autumnaline precursor conversion
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Engineered O-methyltransferase that methylates (S)-12b more efficiently through improved substrate binding
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: engineered bottleneck-relieving replacement for the rate-limiting o-methylation step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: colchicine precursor; pharmaceutical alkaloid pathway intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Greatly improves O-methylation of the autumnaline precursor and increases autumnaline yield 2.25-fold in the cascade
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in E. coli; expression improved by SUMO fusion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: rational design based on AlphaFold2 docking; mutant screening; kinetic assay; whole-cell biotransformation; scale-up validation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Rational engineering identified V125Q as the key beneficial mutation in GsOMT1. 2. Docking and kinetics showed improved substrate binding and catalytic efficiency toward (S)-12b. 3. Replacing RnCOMT with GsOMT1V125Q raised autumnaline production to 2.14 mM in small-scale reactions and supported scale-up to 709.06 mg L−1.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime engineering target for scalable biosynthesis of autumnaline and likely other difficult PEIA substrates
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: plug-and-play replacement in one-pot three-step whole-cell cascade; pH adjustment and SAM supply required
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Expression in E. coli is low without SUMO tag; still not fully optimal and further engineering is suggested
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutation induces a new hydrogen-bonding network among substrate, N181, and Q125.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: endogenous aldehyde reduction side-reaction control affecting precursor retention
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout contributed to reduced conversion of aldehyde intermediate to alcohol by-product, increasing PEIA intermediate accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: genetic deletion to suppress endogenous aldehyde-reduction network
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: endogenous aldehyde/alcohol metabolism genes including yqhD-related system
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: loss of yqhC reduces host aldehyde detoxification capacity
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: native host factor in E. coli
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: host gene knockout in engineered IAA strain; comparison of product/by-product titers by HPLC
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. yqhC was deleted together with six aldehyde-reducing enzyme genes to create the IAA host. 2. This host engineering reduced formation of alcohol by-product 1a' from aldehyde 1a. 3. The engineered host improved accumulation of the productive PEIA intermediate (S)-1b.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Host engineering factor that helps preserve aldehyde precursor and improve pathway flux, but not the principal focus compared with catalytic enzymes.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not individually dissected; deletion may alter native stress responses to aldehydes
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful host engineering target to minimize aldehyde loss in microbial cascades using reactive aldehyde intermediates
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the paper explicitly knocked out this transcription factor as part of the productive host engineering strategy.
